items.question: The past simple of 'buy' is ___.
explanations: 'Buy' is irregular, so it never takes -ed. The past is 'bought'.
Correct! 'Buy → bought → bought'. Same form for past simple and participle.
'Boughted' adds an ending that doesn't exist. The past is just 'bought'.
'Buys' is present simple (he/she/it). The past simple is 'bought'.

items.question: I have ___ my homework.
explanations: 'Did' is the past simple. After 'have', use the past participle: 'done'.
Correct! After have/has, use the participle: 'do → did → done', so 'have done'.
'Doed' isn't a word. The participle of 'do' is 'done'.
'Does' is present simple. After 'have' the form is 'done'.

items.question: She ___ to Italy last year.
explanations: 'Go' is irregular and never takes -ed. The past simple is 'went'.
Correct! 'Go → went → gone'. For a finished action last year, use 'went'.
'Gone' is the past participle (used with have). Alone, use the past simple 'went'.
'Going' needs an auxiliary. A finished action last year takes 'went'.

items.question: Have you ever ___ sushi?
explanations: 'Ate' is the past simple. After 'have', use the participle: 'eaten'.
Correct! 'eat → ate → eaten', and after 'have' you need the participle: 'eaten'.
'Eated' isn't a word. The participle of 'eat' is 'eaten'.
After 'have you ever' you need the participle, not the base form: 'eaten'.

items.question: They ___ the match yesterday.
explanations: 'Win' is irregular and doesn't take -ed. The past is 'won'.
Correct! 'win → won → won'. 'Yesterday' is finished time, so 'won'.
'Wan' isn't a form of this verb. The past simple is 'won'.
'Win' is the base form. A finished action yesterday takes 'won'.

items.question: He has ___ his keys again.
explanations: Correct! 'lose → lost → lost', so after 'has' the form is 'lost'.
'Losed' isn't a word. 'Lose' is irregular: 'lost'.
'Loosed' is a different (rare) verb. The participle of 'lose' is 'lost'.
'Loses' is present simple. After 'has' the form is 'lost'.

items.question: The past participle of 'write' is ___.
explanations: 'Wrote' is the past simple. The past participle is 'written'.
Correct! 'write → wrote → written'. The participle is 'written'.
'Writed' isn't a word — 'write' is irregular.
'Writ' is archaic. Modern English uses 'written'.

items.question: We ___ a great time at the party.
explanations: 'Have' is irregular and doesn't take -ed. The past is 'had'.
Correct! 'have → had → had'. For a finished past event, use 'had'.
'Has' is present simple (he/she/it). With 'we' and a past event, use 'had'.
'Have' is the present form. The party is finished, so use 'had'.

items.question: Which sentence is correct?
explanations: 'Saw' is the past simple. After 'have' you need the participle 'seen'.
Correct! 'see → saw → seen', so 'I have seen the film.'
'See' is the base form. After 'have' use the participle: 'seen'.
'Seed' isn't a form of this verb. The participle of 'see' is 'seen'.

items.question: She ___ her leg while she was skiing.
explanations: 'Break' is irregular and doesn't take -ed. The past simple is 'broke'.
Correct! 'break → broke → broken'. For the past simple, use 'broke'.
'Broken' is the past participle (used with have). Alone, use 'broke'.
'Breaks' is present simple. The action is in the past: 'broke'.
